title: 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships for Duke University Students
summary: The Samuel DuBois Cook Center on Social Equity at Duke University is now accepting applications for its 2026 Summer Research Fellowships. These fellowships are open to both graduate and undergraduate students and are designed to provide hands-on experience in cutting-edge research focused on social inequality. Participants gain the opportunity to deepen their scholarship, expand their professional networks, and actively contribute to the Cook Center’s mission of advancing social equity. The Cook Center conducts interdisciplinary research aimed at understanding the structures and systems that drive inequality in society. Its research pillars include educational and economic opportunity, health equity, carceral justice, and public policy engagement. The Center emphasizes a data-driven approach and methodological innovation, providing students with exposure to advanced research techniques and analytical tools. Fellows are integrated into dynamic research teams where they collaborate with faculty, postdoctoral researchers, graduate and undergraduate students, and other research professionals. Graduate fellows may mentor undergraduate students while simultaneously receiving guidance from senior scholars, ensuring a layered mentorship environment. This collaborative structure promotes leadership development, teamwork, and a comprehensive understanding of research practice in social equity. Why This Opportunity Matters Inequality remains a pressing challenge worldwide. The Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships provide students with the opportunity to investigate the causes and consequences of inequality and to develop evidence-based solutions. Through participation in this program, students gain valuable insight into how research informs policy, social programs, and public debate. The program emphasizes mentorship, collaborative research, and professional development, equipping students with the skills needed to succeed in academia, policy, and advocacy. Fellows gain practical experience, broaden their networks, and enhance their understanding of interdisciplinary approaches to social equity research. These experiences are crucial for students pursuing careers in research, public policy, education, and other fields focused on social justice. By fostering a diverse community of scholars, the Cook Center ensures that participants are prepared to contribute meaningfully to efforts aimed at reducing inequality and promoting social equity both nationally and globally. What You’ll Gain The Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships offer a wide range of professional, academic, and personal benefits: Stipend: Graduate fellows receive $10,625.01 for the summer term, while undergraduate fellows receive $2,500 per term, with the possibility of earning $5,000 if participating in both terms. Mentorship: Individualized guidance from faculty, postdoctoral researchers, and senior scholars provides valuable career support. Research Experience: Fellows gain practical skills by contributing to ongoing research projects, including data collection, literature review, analysis, and research dissemination. Skill Development: Build expertise in research methods, statistical analysis, interdisciplinary collaboration, project management, and communication. Networking: Engage with peers, mentors, and professionals across multiple disciplines, creating opportunities for future collaboration. Professional Exposure: Fellows share research outcomes through the Cook Center website and may gain visibility in academic and professional communities. Graduate fellows will also have the opportunity to mentor undergraduate fellows, fostering leadership and teaching skills, while undergraduates gain exposure to advanced research practices and interdisciplinary approaches to studying inequality. Eligibility Graduate Fellows: Must be current Duke University PhD students in good standing, enrolled for the Fall 2026 semester. Must coordinate with their department to ensure summer funding arrangements are compatible. Undergraduate Fellows: Must be current Duke University student enrolled for Fall 2026. Must be eligible to work in the United States. All Fellows: Must have a strong interest in social equity research and commit to active participation in fellowship activities. Must be able to attend biweekly team meetings and contribute to research projects for the duration of the fellowship. Application Process and Deadline Applicants interested in the 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships must download the appropriate application form for graduate or undergraduate students from the Cook Center website. All required materials, including completed application forms, curriculum vitae, statements of research interests, and letters of recommendation, should be compiled into a single PDF. Applicants will then submit their completed application according to the instructions provided on the official website. Deadlines: Priority consideration: March 31, 2026 Rolling applications: April 30, 2026, depending on availability To learn more and submit your application, visit the official Cook Center website . title: MSc in Olympic Studies 2026–2027: Advance Your Career in Olympic Education
summary: The University of Peloponnese, in partnership with the International Olympic Academy (IOA), invites applications for the Master’s Degree Programme in “Olympic Studies, Olympic Education, Organization and Management of Olympic Events” (MSc) for the academic year 2026–2027. This internationally recognized programme is supported by Olympic Solidarity, an initiative of the International Olympic Committee (IOC), which offers scholarships for selected candidates. The programme is designed for graduates and professionals who want to deepen their knowledge of the Olympic Movement, gain expertise in Olympic philosophy, education, and sport management, and contribute to the global development of sport and Olympic education. Classes are conducted in Ancient Olympia and Sparta, Greece, providing students with a unique immersive experience at the historical heart of Olympism. Through a combination of on-site lectures, practical workshops, and a master’s thesis completed remotely, the MSc offers an interdisciplinary approach, integrating education, sport, culture, and management. Students also benefit from the opportunity to join a global network of professionals and organizations involved in the Olympic Movement. What You’ll Gain Participants in the MSc will gain multiple benefits that advance their academic and professional careers: Specialized Knowledge: Acquire advanced understanding of Olympic philosophy, pedagogy, and the historical and modern Olympic Games. Practical Experience: Learn the organization and management of Olympic events and major sports competitions. Professional Development: Gain skills in sports marketing, sponsorship, strategic planning, and financial management of sports organizations. Global Networking: Connect with Olympic committees, international federations, and professionals engaged in sports education, culture, and event management. Scholarship Opportunities: Tuition and full-boarding scholarships are available through National Olympic Committees for eligible candidates. By combining theory and practical application, this programme prepares graduates to contribute to Olympic education programs, lead sport organizations, and participate actively in the international Olympic Movement. Eligibility Candidates must meet the following criteria: Hold a bachelor’s degree from a recognized Greek or foreign higher education institution. Preference is given to officials of National Olympic Academies, National Olympic Committees, the International Olympic Committee, international and national federations, and researchers in Olympic studies or sport management. Graduates from related fields such as education, sport, management, humanities, journalism, economics, pedagogy, or physical education are also eligible. Candidates must demonstrate professional experience, participation in sport events, Olympic education activities, or related research. Proficiency in English is required, with certificates for other languages considered an asset. Applicants must provide supporting documentation, including transcripts, CV, recommendation letters, identification, proof of participation in relevant activities, and any certifications relevant to their experience. Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed online. Programme Structure The MSc programme runs for three semesters : First Semester (18 Sept – 21 Nov 2026): On-site lectures in Ancient Olympia and Sparta, covering the history and philosophy of sport, modern Olympic Games, Olympic pedagogy, ethics, and research methods. Second Semester (3 Apr – 6 Jun 2027): On-site lectures in Greece, with modules on Olympic pedagogy, organization of games and major sports events, sports marketing, and optional modules such as integrity in sport, financial management, and sport policies. Third Semester (Sept 2027 – Feb 2028): Completion and submission of the master’s thesis, conducted remotely. All lectures are taught in English, and students are required to attend lectures, submit assignments, and complete examinations to progress successfully. Costs and Scholarships Tuition fees for the programme are 5,000 € for the three semesters, payable in three installments. Full-boarding at the IOA premises is approximately 3,500 € , with final costs confirmed individually. Scholarships are available through National Olympic Committees via IOC Olympic Solidarity. Applicants are encouraged to declare interest in scholarships in their application form and contact their National Olympic Committees for further guidance. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must submit the following certified documents both via email and in hard copy to the programme secretariat by 30 April 2026 : Completed application form Bachelor’s degree certificate and transcript, including thesis title and grade CV detailing studies, professional experience, research, and extracurricular activities Language proficiency certificates Passport or ID copy Recommendation letters from professors or relevant sport organizations Supporting documents for professional experience, participation in sports, or Olympic activities 👉 To learn more and apply, visit the official MSc in Olympic Studies programme page . title: World Literacy Foundation Youth Ambassador Program 2026: Empowering Young Leaders for Global Literacy
summary: About the Opportunity The World Literacy Foundation (WLF) Youth Ambassador Program 2026 is a global initiative designed to empower young people to become active leaders in the fight against illiteracy. Through this program, participants aged 16 to 25 will join a worldwide community of changemakers, connect with peers across more than 60 countries, and develop skills to advocate for literacy at local and global levels. WLF is an international non-profit organization dedicated to reducing illiteracy and promoting equitable access to education. Its Youth Ambassador Program is grounded in the principle of youth empowerment, believing that young people’s creativity, vision, and energy are key to achieving meaningful impact. Over a three-month period from June to September 2026, 1000 young leaders will participate in online modules, collaborative projects, and advocacy initiatives designed to give them the tools to make a measurable difference in their communities. The program is entirely online, allowing participants from around the world to join without geographic constraints. Training focuses on leadership, advocacy, fundraising, social media communication, and project implementation. Ambassadors will gain hands-on experience designing campaigns, engaging with communities, and amplifying literacy initiatives. Participants who fundraise for WLF will receive a formal letter of completion, while all participants will receive a certificate recognizing their efforts upon completion of the program. Why This Opportunity Matters Literacy is a foundational skill for education, empowerment, and equitable development. Around the world, millions of children face barriers to learning due to poverty, conflict, or lack of resources. The Youth Ambassador Program enables young people to address these systemic challenges by raising awareness, mobilizing resources, and implementing advocacy campaigns. By participating, ambassadors gain a unique perspective on global literacy issues and learn how to create change that is both local and international. The program not only amplifies youth voices but also cultivates the next generation of global leaders committed to equitable education. In addition, ambassadors contribute to innovative solutions to combat illiteracy, ensuring that knowledge and learning opportunities reach underserved populations. What You’ll Gain The Youth Ambassador Program provides participants with multiple benefits that extend beyond the immediate three-month program: Skill Development: Ambassadors will build leadership, advocacy, communication, public relations, and fundraising skills applicable in both educational and professional contexts. Global Network: Participants join a diverse cohort of 1000 young leaders, fostering international collaboration and shared learning. Real-World Impact: By leading literacy campaigns and fundraising initiatives, ambassadors contribute directly to increasing access to education for vulnerable children globally. Recognition and Visibility: Ambassadors will have opportunities to have their voices amplified through WLF’s website, social media channels, and mainstream media features. Practical Experience in Advocacy: Through youth-led campaigns and creative projects, participants gain first-hand experience in social impact initiatives and community mobilization. This combination of benefits ensures that participants are not only contributing to literacy efforts but are also growing as capable, confident global leaders. Eligibility To participate in the Youth Ambassador Program 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria: Be 16 to 25 years old at the time of the program. Demonstrate a strong interest in literacy, education, human rights, or social impact . Be motivated to join a global community of young changemakers . Have access to a computer or smartphone and a reliable internet connection for online activities. Be active on social media and willing to use their voice to advocate for literacy. Commit to approximately 30 hours over three months to complete ambassador tasks. Be willing to support fundraising initiatives for WLF to expand global literacy programs. This program is inclusive, welcoming applicants from all countries and backgrounds who are passionate about literacy and social change. Application Process and Deadline Applicants begin by visiting the official World Literacy Foundation Ambassador Program page and selecting “Apply Now.” The website provides step-by-step instructions for completing the application form and submitting all required information. Applicants should carefully review the Terms and Conditions before applying, as the program is recurring annually and requires active commitment to the tasks outlined. The program will run from June to September 2026 , and applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Early submission is encouraged to secure a spot in the global cohort. To learn more and apply, visit the official World Literacy Foundation website . title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific
summary: The University of Queensland (UQ) is offering four fully funded PhD scholarships as part of the ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship , focused on co-developing solutions to climate-driven loss and damage in the Pacific. This is a unique opportunity to work closely with communities, governments, and regional partners to address the social, cultural, and justice dimensions of climate change. These scholarships are ideal for candidates passionate about applied research and committed to creating meaningful change for Pacific Island communities. Why Apply? PhD candidates will engage in research that directly impacts communities in the Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu , offering a rare combination of academic, field, and policy experience. Key benefits include: Fully funded scholarship covering tuition fees, living stipend (AU$37,500 per annum, tax-free), and Overseas Student Health Cover Access to world-class facilities and a dynamic, interdisciplinary research environment at the School of the Environment Funded fieldwork and collaboration with NGOs, regional organizations, and government agencies Mentorship from leading experts, including Professor Karen McNamara , Principal Supervisor Research Focus PhD projects will be co-designed with your advisory team to align with the Fellowship’s objectives: Develop and adapt methods for assessing climate-driven loss and damage Document lived experiences and community-led responses Implement and evaluate locally grounded interventions Inform policy and practice through multi-scalar insights and best-practice guidelines Projects are flexible and tailored to your research interests, disciplinary background, and the needs of partner communities. Who Should Apply Ideal candidates will have academic achievement in: Geography Environmental science Indigenous studies Development studies or related fields Experience or interest in climate adaptation, participatory research, or policy engagement is highly desirable. Scholars from Pacific Island nations, particularly Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu , are encouraged to apply. How to Submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) Submitting an EOI is the first step toward securing your PhD scholarship. Here’s what you need to know: Step 1: Prepare Your EOI Include the following details: Proposed supervisor Scholarship details Previous academic qualifications Referee contacts (minimum two) English language proficiency information Step 2: Upload Required Documents Academic curriculum vitae (CV) Academic transcripts and degree certificates English language documents (if applicable) Formatting Guidelines: PDF format only File name: LASTNAME_firstname_document-name.pdf Include both original and official English translations if documents are in another language Step 3: Academic CV Your CV should focus on academic and research achievements, including: Personal details (name, contact, nationality, languages, ORCID ID) Education and academic awards Employment history and research experience Research outputs, publications, and grants Optional: a 200-word contextual statement on achievements relative to opportunities Do not include photographs, hobbies, or unrelated personal details. Step 4: Referee Details Provide at least two referees who can speak to your academic or research abilities. UQ will contact referees directly; do not submit pre-written letters. After Submitting Your EOI Your nominated supervisor will review your EOI (response typically within two weeks). If the supervisor is interested, an informal interview may be arranged to discuss your research fit. If supported, you will be invited to submit a final application , including: Passport copy Additional documents (proof of citizenship, name change, previous research studies) UQ-approved agent submission for some international applicants If your EOI is not supported, you may choose another supervisor and submit a new EOI. Deadline All EOIs must be submitted by 15 April, 2026 . Early submission is strongly recommended. How to Apply To start your application, visit the official UQ project page: UQ PhD Projects – Climate-Driven Loss and Damage APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . Deadline: 15 April, 2026 For questions about the suitability of the project, contact Professor Karen McNamara at karen.mcnamara@uq.edu.au . Submit your expression of interest (EOI) via UQ PhD Projects. The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action
summary: A Major EU Funding Opportunity for Civil Society Organisations in Ethiopia The Delegation of the European Union to Ethiopia has opened a significant new funding opportunity for civil society organisations through the call titled “Supporting CSO role in sustainable and inclusive development and in Global Gateway Investments.” This initiative is designed to strengthen Ethiopian civil society organisations as independent, resilient, and accountable actors that can contribute to inclusive governance, equitable socio-economic development, and the implementation of Global Gateway investments in the country. This call is part of the CSOs Thematic Programme 2021–2027 and builds on earlier EU support to civil society in Ethiopia. The programme reflects the EU’s broader commitment to democratic participation, rights-based development, and stronger civic engagement, while also linking civil society action to priority investment sectors under the Global Gateway strategy. What This EU Call for Proposals Supports The overall objective of the call is to strengthen Ethiopian civil society organisations so they can play a stronger role in inclusive governance and sustainable development, while also contributing to Global Gateway investments. The call is divided into two separate lots , and applicants may apply under one lot only . For either lot, proposals are expected to use an integrated approach and address at least three priority areas under the selected lot rather than presenting isolated activities. This means applicants need to design proposals that are coherent, strategic, and clearly linked to measurable results. The guidelines also stress the importance of strong intervention logic, relevant indicators, monitoring plans, and meaningful partnerships between consortium members. Funding Available: Budget, Lots, and Grant Size The total indicative budget available under this call is EUR 5,829,000 . The funding is split equally across the two lots, with EUR 2,914,500 allocated to Lot 1 and EUR 2,914,500 allocated to Lot 2. The guidelines note that the Lot 2 allocation is tied to the EU budget for 2027 and is therefore subject to the prior availability of those funds. For both lots, the grant size is fixed at EUR 2,914,500 , meaning this is a high-value funding opportunity aimed at large-scale actions. The EU contribution must represent 95% of the total eligible costs of the action , with the remaining balance financed from other sources unless the applicant can justify full financing as essential for implementation. Lot 1: Strengthening Civil Society Capacity and Civic Space Lot 1 focuses on strengthening civil society’s role, capacity, and enabling environment in Ethiopia. Its specific objective is to improve the capacity, sustainability, and influence of local and grassroots CSOs so they can engage more effectively in policy-making, advocacy, dialogue with authorities, monitoring, and accountability processes at both local and national levels. Under this lot, priority areas include improving CSOs’ ability to influence public policy, support evidence-based advocacy, promote transparency and accountability, and build stronger partnerships and coalitions. It also supports inclusive social and economic development initiatives, stronger community engagement, and better internal governance, sustainability, fundraising, and organisational effectiveness. Another major focus is the protection of civic space and the promotion of an enabling legal and policy environment for civil society. The call also places strong emphasis on Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) under Lot 1. Applicants are expected to establish and manage at least one open and competitive support mechanism for local and grassroots CSOs, including capacity building, mentoring, and tailored technical support. At least EUR 1,860,000 must be reserved for FSTP to local and grassroots CSOs, and at least EUR 291,000 must be allocated to strengthening the capacities of those recipients. Lot 2: Civil Society Engagement in Global Gateway Priority Sectors Lot 2 is aimed at strengthening civil society engagement in Global Gateway priority sectors in Ethiopia. Its objective is to enhance the role and capacity of CSOs to contribute to transparent, inclusive, and sustainable policy-making, reform processes, and investment processes in four sectors: clean energy transition, sustainable agri-food value chains, health systems strengthening, and human-centric digital transformation . Priority areas under Lot 2 include improving governance and regulatory frameworks, increasing transparency and accountability in investment processes, empowering communities to participate in decision-making, and supporting inclusive skills development and economic opportunities linked to investment sectors. The call encourages actions that strengthen public oversight, promote environmental and social safeguards, support grievance mechanisms, and expand the role of communities and local CSOs across the investment cycle. Actions under Lot 2 must be implemented in at least three specific locations in Ethiopia where Global Gateway projects are already being implemented , and applicants must clearly explain how their chosen areas connect to relevant Global Gateway investments. Who Is Eligible to Apply? This opportunity is intended for non-profit legal entities such as civil society organisations, including NGOs, networks, and associations. To qualify, the lead applicant must be a legal person, be non-profit-making, and be directly responsible for the preparation and management of the action. Eligible lead applicants must be effectively established in an EU Member State, Ethiopia, or another country eligible under Article 28 of the NDICI-Global Europe regulation . A key requirement is that the lead applicant must apply together with at least two co-applicants established and registered in Ethiopia . In addition, the consortium must demonstrate experience managing at least one EU grant above EUR 1.5 million in the past three years , at least three years of practical experience working with local CSOs and building their capacity , and a proven track record in managing Financial Support to Third Parties schemes. For lead applicants not established in Ethiopia, the rules are stricter. They must demonstrate prior knowledge of Ethiopia’s civil society landscape, show that they have implemented at least two projects with local CSOs , and prove that local CSOs have been involved in the conception, governance, implementation, monitoring, and reporting of the proposed action. Project Duration, Locations, and Design Requirements For both lots, the initial planned duration of an action must be at least 36 months and no more than 48 months . For Lot 1, actions must take place in at least three locations from the following exclusive list: Somali, Harari, Sidama, Gambella, South West Ethiopia, and Dire Dawa City Administration . Beyond location and duration, the guidelines require all proposals to demonstrate strong internal coherence between needs, objectives, expected results, activities, and indicators. Applicants are strongly encouraged to define one clear specific objective, include a solid monitoring plan, and describe genuine, balanced partnerships among consortium members. The call also includes mandatory cross-cutting priorities. All proposals must integrate meaningful youth engagement , a human rights-based approach , gender equality and women’s empowerment , and conflict sensitivity throughout the design and implementation of the action. How to Apply Through PROSPECT and PADOR This is a restricted call for proposals , which means the first stage requires the submission of a concept note only . Applicants whose concept notes are pre-selected will later be invited to submit a full application. Applications must be submitted online via PROSPECT , and organisations must register in PADOR . In addition, lead applicants and co-applicants must register in the European Commission’s Participant Register . The guidelines strongly recommend completing these registration steps well in advance rather than waiting until the final days before submission. Applicants must submit their concept notes in English , follow the official application format carefully, and ensure that the concept note contains all relevant information because no extra annexes are evaluated at that first stage. The full application stage, for those invited, will require more detailed documentation, including the budget, logical framework, and supporting eligibility documents. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Deadline and Key Dates The deadline for submission of concept notes is 13 May 2026 at 15:30 Brussels time . This is the most important immediate deadline for interested applicants. There will also be an information session on 17 April 2026 in hybrid format. Organisations interested in attending must send an email by 8 April 2026 with the requested participant details. According to the indicative timetable, applicants can expect the following milestones: information to pre-selected applicants and invitations to submit full applications: 25 June 2026 deadline for full applications: 11 August 2026 at 15:30 Brussels time notification of contracting authority decision: 20 October 2026 indicative contract signature: 20 November 2026 Final Tips Before You Apply For organisations considering this opportunity, early preparation will be essential. Because the call requires consortium formation, registration on multiple EU platforms, and a strong concept note aligned with one of the two lots, applicants should begin preparing as soon as possible. It is especially important to select the correct lot from the beginning, build a proposal with a strong and coherent intervention logic, and make sure the consortium’s experience clearly matches the requirements set out in the guidelines. For organisations applying under Lot 1, the design of the FSTP component will be particularly important. For Lot 2, applicants must be precise in linking their action to actual Global Gateway project locations in Ethiopia. For civil society organisations with the right structure, partnerships, and implementation capacity, this call represents a major opportunity to secure substantial EU support for long-term impact in Ethiopia. The post EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships
summary: Leeds University Business School (LUBS) is proud to announce its Post Doctoral Fellowship Scheme, designed to accelerate the careers of early-stage researchers. This two-year fellowship provides a structured and supportive environment, enabling scholars to build world-class research portfolios and gain valuable academic experience. The fellowships are available across multiple disciplines including Accounting Finance, Analytics, Technology Operations, Economics, International Business, Management Organisations, Marketing, and People, Work Employment Relations. This initiative reflects LUBS’s commitment to strengthening research capacity and fostering impactful scholarship. Fellowship Benefits Fellows at LUBS receive: Structured training programs tailored for early-career researchers Dedicated mentoring on publications, funding applications, and career development Opportunities for teaching experience across a variety of disciplines Access to a supportive research community and cohort network Additional benefits include excellent annual leave entitlement, generous pension schemes, family-friendly policies, fitness and wellbeing facilities, and extensive professional development opportunities. Eligibility Requirements PhD completed or near completion in a relevant field Research experience through journals, conferences, or similar platforms, with strong communication skills Teaching experience and ability to work independently and collaboratively Strong qualitative or quantitative methodological skills with advanced research training Commitment to ongoing professional development Application Process Applicants must engage with a potential mentor who is a current faculty member in the relevant department to discuss research plans and secure a supporting statement. This step is essential for a successful application. Interested candidates can apply via the following links: Accounting Finance Department Analytics, Technology and Operations Department Economics Department International Business Department Marketing Department Management and Organisation Department People, Work and Employment Department APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Important Dates Release Date: 31 March 2026 Closing Date: 31 May 2026 For international applicants, eligibility under the Skilled Worker visa or Global Talent visa may apply. Further details can be found on UK Government Skilled Worker visa and Global Talent visa pages. The post Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026
summary: Are you a recent graduate passionate about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science? Do you aspire to use your technical skills to tackle global challenges and drive social impact? The Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 is your gateway to hands-on experience in creating innovative data solutions for real-world development problems. About Dalberg Data Insights Dalberg Data Insights (DDI) bridges the AI adoption gap in Low- and Middle-Income Countries (LMICs), ensuring no population is left behind in the digital transformation. As part of the global Dalberg Group , DDI designs AI strategies, develops solutions, provides AI literacy training, and pilots responsible AI initiatives for impactful organizations. Their proprietary AI platform, augment.impact , enables the creation of advanced AI analysts and advisors (aia2), helping clients efficiently transform data into actionable insights. The AI Engineering Fellowship Program The 6-month fellowship is designed to develop future leaders in AI and data science. Selected fellows will: Rotate through roles including data engineer, data scientist, and Gen AI specialist Contribute to building data pipelines, AI solutions, and visualization tools Participate in code reviews and improve technical processes Drive initiatives to enhance workflows and implement innovative solutions While full-time employment is not guaranteed, high-performing fellows may be offered permanent positions within Dalberg. Why Join the Fellowship? The program offers unmatched professional and technical development: Technical Mastery: Build advanced skills in AI, data engineering, and data science Professional Growth: Understand project responsibilities, explore your strengths, and align with organizational needs Impactful Work: Contribute to AI-driven solutions for global challenges such as pandemics, climate change, and youth unemployment Who Should Apply Ideal candidates are curious, resourceful, and self-driven. You should: Have a strong background in AI, Data Science, or related fields Be proficient in Python and other programming languages Thrive in multicultural teams and fast-paced environments Be authorized to work in Kenya Key Dates Application Deadline: April 17, 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is encouraged. Join Dalberg Data Insights If you’re ready to launch your career in AI and data for social impact, apply for the AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 in Nairobi, Kenya. Transform your skills into solutions that drive global change. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . title: UNODC Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Crime Research – Work on Global Security from Dakar
summary: The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime is offering an exceptional opportunity for young professionals to launch an impactful international career. Through the United Nations Junior Professional Officer Programme , aspiring researchers can contribute to cutting-edge work addressing organized crime, terrorism, and drug-related challenges in West and Central Africa. 📌 About the Opportunity This Junior Professional Officer (JPO) position is based in Dakar, Senegal, within UNODC’s Regional Office for West and Central Africa. It is designed for early-career researchers passionate about data, policy, and international development. In this role, you will work at the intersection of research and policy, supporting evidence-based strategies to combat transnational crime and strengthen justice systems across the region. 🎯 Key Responsibilities As a JPO in Crime Research, you will: Conduct research on transnational organized crime, terrorism, and drug trafficking Analyze complex datasets to generate actionable insights Contribute to high-level UNODC regional and global reports Support capacity-building initiatives in Member States Participate in field missions across West and Central Africa 🎓 Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this role, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be 32 years old or younger by 31 December 2026 Hold an advanced degree in: Statistics Sociology Criminology Economics Or related social sciences Have: At least 2 years of research experience , OR 4 years of experience with a bachelor’s degree Demonstrate fluency in both English and French Be a national of an eligible country (see full list in official job description) 🌍 Why This Opportunity Matters This role offers more than just a job—it’s a gateway to a meaningful international career: 🌐 Work with a leading UN agency tackling global crime issues 📊 Gain hands-on experience in high-impact research and data analysis ✈️ Participate in international missions and fieldwork 🤝 Collaborate with global experts and policymakers 🚀 Build a strong foundation for future UN or international roles 📝 Application Process The JPO recruitment process is managed through donor countries participating in the UN JPO Programme. Here’s how it works: Donor countries publish available JPO vacancies Candidates apply through their respective national JPO portals Shortlisted applicants are reviewed and interviewed by UN offices Final selection is made by the hosting UN entity Because of this structure, candidates should regularly check their country’s official JPO programme website or Ministry of Foreign Affairs. ⏳ Application Deadline 26 April 2026 – 6:00 PM (EST) Make sure to apply before the deadline through the appropriate national JPO channel. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . 🚀 Start Your Global Career With over 350 JPOs currently serving across more than 50 UN departments worldwide, this programme offers a proven pathway into the United Nations system. If you are passionate about research, global security, and making a tangible impact, this opportunity with UNODC could be your next big step. title: Africa IFI Community Grants 2026: Up to £1,500 Funding for Education Projects Across Africa
summary: Applications are now open for the Africa IFI Community Grants 2026 , an initiative designed to support community-based organizations across Africa that are improving education outcomes for children and young people. This grant program offers funding of up to £1,500 GBP per project , aiming to ensure fair, transparent, and equitable distribution of financial support to grassroots initiatives. The number of grants awarded will depend on the availability of funds in each round. Grant Timeline The application cycle for the Africa IFI Community Grants 2026 is as follows: Applications Open: 30 March 2026 Applications Close: 13 April 2026 Successful Applicants Notified: To Be Determined (approximately 1–2 months after closing) About the Grant Program The Africa IFI grant portfolio is committed to empowering locally driven organizations that are making a direct impact within their communities. The program prioritizes projects that focus on improving access to quality education for children and youth up to the age of 25. By supporting grassroots organizations, the initiative aims to strengthen community-led solutions and promote long-term educational development across the continent. Funding Details Grant amount: Up to £1,500 GBP Coverage: Projects across African countries Allocation: Based on funding availability per round Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this grant, applicants must meet the following requirements: The organization must be operating within an African country The proposed project must focus on improving education for children or young adults up to age 25 The organization must have been operational for at least 12 months The organization must be community-based Responsibilities of Successful Applicants Organizations selected for funding will be required to: Implement the proposed project within the agreed timeframe Submit a detailed report within 3 months after project completion Clearly outline project outcomes and how funds were utilized Important Notes Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered Incomplete applications will be automatically disqualified Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all requirements before submission Deadline All applications must be submitted by 13 April 2026 . Early applications are strongly encouraged to avoid disqualification due to last-minute errors. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . title: Transform Your Philanthropic Impact: Join the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026
summary: Introduction If you’re ready to take your philanthropic work to the next level and collaborate with influential leaders across Asia, the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain world-class insights, build strategic partnerships, and drive measurable impact. Delivered by AVPN Academy in partnership with the Cambridge Judge Business School, this hybrid fellowship blends virtual learning with an immersive, in-person experience in New Delhi. Programme Overview The AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship is designed to equip professionals and changemakers with the tools needed to deploy philanthropic capital effectively for sustainable and scalable outcomes. The programme focuses on aligning philanthropic efforts with global development priorities, including the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Participants will explore real-world case studies, learn strategic frameworks, and engage with a global network of peers committed to advancing impactful philanthropy. Key Programme Dates 16 April 2026 – Virtual induction and kickoff 20–23 April 2026 – Virtual learning sessions 24–27 August 2026 – In-person convening in New Delhi Application Deadline: 3 April 2026 (or until seats are filled) What You’ll Learn The fellowship covers a wide range of essential themes, including: Strategic deployment of philanthropic capital Building and managing multi-stakeholder partnerships Scaling impact through sustainable models Governance and leadership in philanthropy Measuring and evaluating long-term impact Participants will gain insights into transitioning from traditional to strategic philanthropy, leveraging branding for greater influence, and aligning initiatives with national and global development frameworks. Programme Benefits Access to expert-led workshops and executive education Certificate of attendance Networking with global leaders and peers Complimentary ticket to the AVPN Global Conference 2026 Hands-on learning through real-world case studies Leadership and strategic thinking development Programme Format This is a hybrid programme combining: Virtual Learning: Interactive modules delivered over four days In-Person Experience: A four-day convening alongside the AVPN Global Conference in New Delhi Programme Fees USD 2,000 for AVPN members USD 3,000 for non-members Complimentary for AVPN Suite Pass subscribers Note: Travel and accommodation costs for the in-person convening are not included. Who Should Apply This fellowship is ideal for individuals and professionals who are actively involved in or transitioning into the philanthropic sector, including: Philanthropy practitioners and advisors Corporate professionals driving social impact Policymakers and government officials Academics and researchers Individual philanthropists and foundation leaders Why You Should Apply The AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship is more than a learning programme—it is a gateway to becoming part of a transformative community shaping the future of giving in Asia and beyond. With limited seats available, this is a competitive and high-impact opportunity for those serious about driving change. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply as soon as possible, as only shortlisted applicants will be notified and spaces are limited. APPLY HERE.
